summary: Matched and linked src/melee/ft/ftanim.c by replacing nonmatching stubs with C implementations, correcting ftanim prototypes and caller argument order, adding reusable texture-object-list typing, and switching ftanim.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The PR also made small cleanup/name improvements around fighter animation fields, Fighter_Part arguments, AOBJ flags, and ftAnim tree traversal symbol names. No human review comments were present in the slice; the only feedback was the decomp.dev report showing +3304 matched code bytes and +13364 linked code bytes.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ftanim.c") became Object(Matching, "melee/ft/ftanim.c"), linking the whole ftanim unit.
Renamed two ftanim symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from address-style names to behavior names: ftAnim_GetNextAnimJointInTree and ftAnim_GetNextMatAnimJointInTree.

Implemented many previously nonmatching ftanim functions, including texture animation helpers and part animation helpers such as ftAnim_80070200, ftAnim_80070458, ftAnim_800705E0, ftAnim_80070A10, ftAnim_80070C48, ftAnim_80070CC4, and ftAnim_80070E74.

Refined ftanim.h prototypes to use recovered types: Fighter_Part instead of raw int for part indices, bool for boolean parameters, ftData_x8_x8 for fighter data texture animation metadata, and CostumeTObjList for costume texture object state.
Added struct CostumeTObjList to src/melee/ft/dobjlist.h and forward-declared it in src/melee/ft/forward.h.
Moved Fighter texture-object fields n_costume_tobjs, x5D0, and costume_tobjs[5] into Fighter.tobj_list as CostumeTObjList.
Updated Kirby fighter vars so ftKb_FighterVars.x44 is CostumeTObjList instead of a placeholder struct KirbyFV_x44_t, and adjusted ftKb_Init.c calls/casts accordingly.
Renamed Fighter.x8A8_unk to x8A8_anim_frame and Fighter.x594_b1 to x594_b1_loop in src/melee/ft/types.h.
Changed call sites in common fall, guard, Sheik special side, Yoshi guard, and related fighter files to pass FtPart_TransN rather than literal 1 when invoking ftanim part APIs.
Corrected some ftanim call argument order at callers and prototypes, notably ftAnim_80070010 and ftAnim_80070108 now taking float blend parameters before the HSD_Joint*.
Replaced magic AObj flag constants such as 0x20000000 and bit masks with AOBJ_LOOP and ~AOBJ_NO_ANIM in fighter/stage/baselib code.
Replaced explicit OSReport plus __assert patterns in ftanim.c with HSD_ASSERTREPORT while preserving assertion messages and source line constants.
Used HSD_JObjGetChild, HSD_JObjGetNext, and HSD_JObjGetParent accessors in ftanim tree traversal instead of local child/next/parent wrappers.
smart_moves: Recovered a real aggregate type, CostumeTObjList, then used it both inside Fighter and Kirby fighter vars; this avoided carrying an opaque Kirby-specific placeholder and made texture-animation helper signatures reusable.
Used named enums/constants at call sites, especially FtPart_TransN and AOBJ_LOOP, which clarified semantics while also helping enforce the corrected function signatures.

Split texture-object setup and animation operations into typed helpers ftAnim_80070200, ftAnim_80070458, and ftAnim_800705E0, reducing duplicated inline logic and making the matched implementation easier to reason about.
Kept matching-sensitive helper shapes where needed: the diff explicitly notes scale_inline is load-bearing to fix float order, and TODOs mark suspected inlines that were not fully forced.
Improved symbol names only where behavior was clear from traversal functions, avoiding overnaming most still-address-style ftAnim functions.
Updated external callers together with prototype changes, preventing header drift after correcting part-index and blend-parameter semantics.
decomp_lessons: When matching animation code, raw integer part arguments should be pressure-tested against Fighter_Part; replacing literals like 1 with FtPart_TransN can expose and document actual API semantics.
Texture animation state shared across Fighter and character-specific vars can often be recovered as a small reusable struct rather than separate placeholder fields.
Matching C may require apparently minor helper extraction to control generated float operation order; in this PR, scale_inline was documented as load-bearing.

Argument order in prototypes should be validated against call-site behavior and matching output, not just guessed from current nonmatching declarations; several ftanim blend helpers changed parameter ordering.
Prefer existing symbolic flags such as AOBJ_LOOP, AOBJ_NO_ANIM, AOBJ_ARG_AU, and JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION over magic constants once their meanings are known.
For tree-walking helpers, behavior names like GetNextAnimJointInTree are high-value because they encode traversal semantics and improve future grepability.
Replacing OSReport plus __assert with HSD_ASSERTREPORT can preserve assert behavior while producing better matching code in this codebase.
Field renames such as x8A8_anim_frame are useful when the field's role is proven by matched animation blend code.
naming_conventions: Use ftAnim_GetNextAnimJointInTree, ftAnim_GetNextMatAnimJointInTree, and ftAnim_GetNextJointInTree for depth-first tree traversal helpers rather than address-only names.
Use Fighter_Part for fighter part indices in ftanim APIs; use FtPart_TransN at trans-N/root-part call sites instead of literal 1.
Use bool for flag-like ftanim parameters instead of int where the implementation treats the value as boolean.
Name recovered texture object aggregate CostumeTObjList and store it as Fighter.tobj_list.
Use descriptive field names when behavior is established, e.g. x8A8_anim_frame and x594_b1_loop.
Use AOBJ_LOOP and AOBJ_NO_ANIM for baselib animation flags rather than 0x20000000 or hardcoded inverse masks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed ftanim.c to Matching only after filling remaining nonmatching functions and synchronizing headers/callers.
Used a small non-inline helper scale_inline(float scale, Vec3*) to force the desired float reciprocal/multiply order in ftAnim_8006E054.
Preserved likely inlining uncertainty with comments such as TODO inline ftAnim_8006F7C8 and TODO inline ftAnim_80070C48 rather than overfitting names or structure without proof.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT(line, 0, message) with explicit line constants to match assert/report codegen in ftanim.c.
Replaced local JObj traversal wrappers with HSD_JObjGetChild/GetNext/GetParent accessors, likely aligning codegen and project API usage.
Recovered concrete struct layouts, including CostumeTObjList and ftData_x8_x8, to make pointer arithmetic and field access compile into matching code.
Adjusted function signatures and caller argument order for blend helpers so generated call sequences matched the target.
Introduced unions/alternate byte views for FighterBone flags in types.h, suggesting matching required access to flags both as bitfields and raw hi/lo bytes.
review_feedback: No human review comments or GitHub reviews were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code improved to 48.19% (+0.09%, +3304 bytes), linked code improved to 28.94% (+0.34%, +13364 bytes), and linked data improved to 28.46% (+568 bytes).

decomp.dev listed 13 new ftanim matches, including ftAnim_80070CC4, ftAnim_8006F7C8, ftAnim_80070A10, ftAnim_8006FCE4, ftAnim_80070200, ftAnim_80070010, ftAnim_80070108, ftAnim_80070E74, ftAnim_80070458, ftAnim_80070C48, ftAnim_800705E0, ftAnim_80070308, and ftAnim_80070F28.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining ftAnim_8006xxxx address-style functions after this full ftanim match.
Search for remaining literal FtPart indices in fighter animation call sites that should become Fighter_Part or FtPart_* constants.
Search for remaining magic AObj flags 0x20000000 or 0xBFFFFFFF outside files touched by PR 2067.
Check whether TODO inline ftAnim_8006F7C8 or TODO inline ftAnim_80070C48 were resolved in later matching PRs.
Audit other character-specific placeholder structs that can be replaced by shared recovered structs like CostumeTObjList.
